event_signals.headline: US-Iran war enters second week — Pentagon reports 140 US troops injured in first 10 days
event_signals.summary: The US-Israeli war against Iran (Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion) entered its second week with no ceasefire in sight. The Pentagon confirmed 140 US troops injured, 8 severely, in the first 10 days of fighting. Defense Secretary Hegseth vowed the 'most intense day yet' of US strikes. The White House confirmed boots-on-ground in Iran is 'not ruled out' while Trump offered mixed signals on timeline, predicting both that the war is 'very complete' and entering its most intense phase.

event_signals.headline: Iran begins laying mines in Strait of Hormuz as US Navy suspends escort operations
event_signals.summary: Iran's IRGC began laying naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, further blocking the critical oil chokepoint. Simultaneously, the US Navy informed commercial shipping that Hormuz escorts are 'not possible for now,' leaving tankers without military protection. Energy Secretary Chris Wright posted then deleted a statement about Hormuz. The IRGC declared Tehran will decide when the war ends.

event_signals.headline: Brent crude surges 25% to $116.50/bbl — gold hits $5,195.60 as markets roil
event_signals.summary: Brent crude surged over 25% to $116.50/bbl amid the worsening West Asia conflict, with experts warning of $150/bbl if Hormuz remains blocked. Gold shattered records at $5,195.60/oz. The Dow fell over 800 points and S&P 500 sank despite a brief 13% oil price dip when Trump predicted de-escalation. G7 finance ministers signaled a coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release as the 'fear premium' grips energy markets.

event_signals.headline: G7 finance ministers signal coordinated SPR release amid oil crisis
event_signals.summary: G7 finance ministers discussed oil market stabilization, signaling a coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve release to tamp down surging crude prices. The release would be the largest coordinated SPR drawdown since 2022 and targets the 'fear premium' embedded in energy markets from the Gulf conflict.

event_signals.headline: Hezbollah escalates war — Macron vows to protect Lebanon from conflict spillover
event_signals.summary: Hezbollah continued its 'reckless war' from Lebanon against Israel, endangering the country. French President Macron declared France would act to protect Lebanon from escalation in the Iran war. Analysis suggests Hezbollah's actions are pushing Lebanon toward state failure.

event_signals.headline: US Navy sinks IRIS Dena — Sri Lanka evacuates crew of second Iranian vessel
event_signals.summary: The US Navy sank the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena. Sri Lanka subsequently evacuated the crew of a second Iranian vessel in its waters, indicating expanding naval operations in the Indian Ocean beyond the Persian Gulf theater.

event_signals.headline: US steps up strikes as Trump offers mixed signals — boots on ground 'not ruled out'
event_signals.summary: The White House confirmed US ground troops in Iran are 'not ruled out' while Defense Secretary Hegseth vowed the 'most intense' military strikes yet against Iran. Trump simultaneously offered contradictory timelines — calling the operation 'very complete' while escalating. Kurdish invasion of Iran reportedly receiving US air support. The administration faced criticism for 'doublethink' on war messaging.

event_signals.headline: Trump supports Kurdish invasion of Iran — US jets providing air cover
event_signals.summary: President Trump confirmed US jets would support a Kurdish ground invasion of Iran, opening a new dimension to the conflict through proxy forces. Kurdish groups operating from Iraq's Kurdistan region are advancing into Iranian territory with US air support.

event_signals.headline: Russia caught conducting ship-to-ship oil transfer in Gulf of Oman amid sanctions
event_signals.summary: Russia was caught executing a large-scale 'semi-dark' ship-to-ship oil transfer in the Gulf of Oman, suggesting Moscow is exploiting the Iran crisis to maintain or expand oil trade through shadow fleet operations in contested waters.

event_signals.headline: US says it wouldn't be 'happy' if Russia providing Iran with intelligence
event_signals.summary: The US warned it would not be 'happy' if Russia were providing intelligence to Iran during the conflict, signaling concerns about Russian support for Tehran. This could complicate US-Russia relations and the parallel Ukraine peace track.

event_signals.headline: Ukraine retakes territory as Russia's buffer zone strategy falters
event_signals.summary: Ukraine reportedly retook territory as Russia's buffer zone strategy in border regions faltered. Both sides claimed front-line progress with US-brokered trilateral peace talks on hold. The conflict continues as a frozen/active war while the world's attention pivots to Iran.

event_signals.headline: US proposes fresh trilateral peace talks on Ukraine — Zelensky confirms
event_signals.summary: The US proposed a fresh round of trilateral peace talks next week involving Washington, Kyiv, and Moscow. Zelensky confirmed the proposal. Talks come as both sides claim battlefield gains and the US is distracted by the Iran war.

event_signals.headline: EU transfers €90 billion to Kyiv bypassing Hungary's veto
event_signals.summary: The EU cleared a mechanism to transfer €90 billion to Ukraine by stepping over Hungary's veto through an enhanced cooperation framework. This is a significant institutional precedent for EU decision-making when a single member state blocks consensus.

event_signals.headline: Ukraine accuses Hungary of detaining bank employees with cash shipment
event_signals.summary: Ukraine accused Hungary of detaining Ukrainian bank employees along with a cash shipment, escalating tensions between the two nations. Kyiv described it as taking 'seven people hostage.' The incident further strains already fraught Ukraine-Hungary relations.

event_signals.headline: Volkswagen to slash 50,000 jobs in Germany amid profit decline
event_signals.summary: Volkswagen announced plans to cut 50,000 jobs in Germany, citing sinking profits and competitive pressure from Chinese EV manufacturers. This is the largest single job-cut announcement in German automotive history and reflects structural decline in Europe's largest industrial sector.

event_signals.headline: Kristi Noem fired from DHS — Trump creates 'Shield of the Americas' envoy role
event_signals.summary: President Trump fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem, reassigning her to a new 'Shield of the Americas' Western Hemisphere security envoy position. The move came amid criticism of immigration policy execution. A combative confirmation hearing for her replacement drew attention. The shake-up signals internal White House policy disputes.

event_signals.headline: India approves limited easing of Chinese investment curbs after years of friction
event_signals.summary: India approved a limited easing of restrictions on Chinese foreign direct investment, partially reversing curbs imposed after the 2020 Galwan border clash. The move signals cautious diplomatic thaw between the world's two most populous nations, driven partly by India's need for Chinese manufacturing investment and technology.

event_signals.headline: India export cargo worth ₹346 crore to Iran stranded at Kandla and Mundra ports
event_signals.summary: Indian export cargo worth approximately $40 million destined for Iran is stranded at Kandla and Mundra ports due to the conflict. The government confirmed the situation to parliament, highlighting the direct trade disruption from the Gulf war beyond just oil.

event_signals.headline: China vows to accelerate technological self-reliance and AI push at 'Two Sessions'
event_signals.summary: At the annual 'Two Sessions' parliamentary meetings, China's leadership vowed to accelerate technological self-reliance with a major push into AI. One-person 'claw-powered' AI companies became a hot topic. The declarations reinforce China's decoupling strategy from Western technology dependencies.

event_signals.headline: Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai accepts 20-year sentence — will not appeal
event_signals.summary: Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai, founder of Apple Daily, decided not to appeal his conviction and 20-year prison term under the National Security Law. The British citizen's case has been a major point of contention in UK-China relations.

event_signals.headline: Petro's leftist coalition tops Colombia Congressional elections
event_signals.summary: President Petro's leftist Pacto Histórico coalition won the most seats in Colombia's congressional elections, strengthening his governing mandate. The result gives Petro greater legislative power to advance his reform agenda including land reform, tax changes, and energy transition policies.

event_signals.headline: Paloma Valencia surges in Colombia presidential race as 2026 election season begins
event_signals.summary: Conservative senator Paloma Valencia emerged as a leading contender in Colombia's upcoming presidential race, reshaping the political landscape as the 2026 election season kicks off.

event_signals.headline: Mexico's El Mencho (CJNG cartel leader) killed by military forces
event_signals.summary: Mexican military forces killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ('El Mencho'), leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), one of the world's most powerful drug trafficking organizations. A Mexican general rejected claims that El Mencho had surrendered, stating he 'had a lot of weaponry' when confronted.

event_signals.headline: Trump hints at 'friendly takeover' of Cuba as crisis deepens
event_signals.summary: Trump stated Cuba is 'running on fumes' and suggested a US 'friendly takeover' is closer than expected, signaling potential for escalated pressure or intervention in the Caribbean island nation amid its ongoing economic crisis.

event_signals.headline: EU rails against Russian return to Venice Biennale art festival
event_signals.summary: The EU protested Russia's return to the Venice Biennale art festival, one of the world's most prestigious cultural events. The move highlights the ongoing cultural and diplomatic isolation of Russia in Europe over the Ukraine war.

event_signals.headline: EU admits 'nuclear blunder' — energy policy rethink underway
event_signals.summary: Senior EU officials acknowledged a 'nuclear blunder' in energy policy, with the Iran oil crisis intensifying pressure to reconsider nuclear power as part of Europe's energy security strategy. EU countries were also urged to lower energy taxes to reduce consumer bills.

event_signals.headline: Companies sue for Trump tariff refunds after SCOTUS ruling — $175B windfall
event_signals.summary: Multiple companies filed lawsuits seeking refunds on Trump tariffs after a Supreme Court ruling reshaped the tariff landscape. The total potential refund pool is estimated at $175 billion. Senate Democrats also introduced a bill to shield small businesses from tariff impacts.

event_signals.headline: Oil price volatility — 13% drop on Trump de-escalation prediction, then recovery
event_signals.summary: Crude oil prices swung wildly, dropping 13% after Trump predicted Middle East de-escalation, before recovering as fighting continued. The extreme volatility (>20% intraday range) reflects the market's sensitivity to every geopolitical signal from Washington and Tehran.

event_signals.headline: US Dow falls 800+ points as Iran war jitters persist despite oil dip
event_signals.summary: The Dow Jones fell more than 800 points, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also sinking as Iran war jitters persisted even as oil prices briefly dipped. The sell-off reflects growing recession fears and the market's view that the conflict will be prolonged.

event_signals.headline: 5 recession warning signs emerge in US economy amid oil shock
event_signals.summary: Multiple analysts identified 5 warning signs of a potential US recession including surging energy costs, tightening consumer budgets, bond market stress (leveraged basis trade unwinding), falling consumer confidence, and supply chain disruptions from the Gulf conflict.

event_signals.headline: Bond market stress — leveraged basis trade starts to unwind
event_signals.summary: A hidden leveraged trade in US Treasury bonds — the 'basis trade' — began unwinding amid market stress from the Iran crisis. This $1+ trillion trade involves hedge funds exploiting small price differences between Treasury futures and cash bonds with high leverage. Its unwind recalls the March 2020 Treasury market seizure.

event_signals.headline: ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih operating at loss — high energy costs and CBAM pressure
event_signals.summary: ArcelorMittal's Kryvyi Rih steel plant in Ukraine is operating at a loss due to high energy prices, with the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) adding further pressure. The CEO warned of potential shutdowns.

event_signals.headline: Australia dollar rises to best levels since 2023 — four reasons for strength
event_signals.summary: The Australian dollar surged to its highest level since 2023 against the USD, driven by rising commodity prices (iron ore, LNG, gold), a hawkish RBA, China stimulus, and safe-haven commodity currency flows amid the Middle East crisis.

event_signals.headline: South Africa MGO price hits unprecedented high amid Gulf disruption
event_signals.summary: South Africa's marine gas oil (MGO) price hit an unprecedented high due to global oil market disruption from the Middle East conflict, impacting shipping costs and the broader South African economy.

event_signals.headline: Argentina President Milei woos Wall Street — cites economic turnaround with net energy exports
event_signals.summary: Argentine President Milei courted Wall Street investors, highlighting Argentina's achievement of net energy exports for the first time — particularly significant as the Gulf crisis drives global energy prices higher. Vaca Muerta shale production is boosting export revenues.

event_signals.headline: Gabon's IMF loan push leads regional return to Fund financing in Africa
event_signals.summary: Gabon's uncertain push for an IMF loan is part of a broader trend of African nations returning to the Fund for financing amid tightening global financial conditions and rising energy costs. The post-coup government is seeking to stabilize its economy.

event_signals.headline: Ghana parliament revives dangerous anti-LGBT bill
event_signals.summary: Ghana's parliament revived a controversial anti-LGBT bill that could impose severe criminal penalties. The legislation risks triggering sanctions from Western donors and could affect Ghana's access to World Bank and IMF concessional financing.

event_signals.headline: Informal OTS Foreign Minister meeting tests limits of Turkic coordination
event_signals.summary: An informal meeting of Organization of Turkic States foreign ministers tested the limits of Turkic coordination amid the Iran crisis, as Turkey navigates between its NATO membership and its relationships with Central Asian states and Iran.

event_signals.headline: US midterm mood favors Democrats as GOP faces Iran war and Noem fallout
event_signals.summary: Multiple polls show the midterm mood favoring Democrats despite party image problems. Georgia voters chose a successor to Marjorie Taylor Greene. Sen. Rand Paul warned Republicans are heading toward 'disastrous' midterms if the Iran war continues. The GOP is in 'course-correction mode' after the Noem ouster and unfavorable polls.

event_signals.headline: US-Israeli missile strike near Iranian girls' school draws condemnation
event_signals.summary: A US-Israeli missile strike near a girls' school in Iran drew international condemnation. Trump stated the US is 'willing to live with' the report, generating backlash. WHO verified 13 attacks on health sites in Iran. The civilian toll is rising and generating international pressure for ceasefire.

event_signals.headline: Tehran under heavy US-Israeli bombing — residents describe 'hearts shaking'
event_signals.summary: Tehran residents described enduring heavy Israeli-US bombing with 'hearts shaking.' The capital city is under sustained aerial assault. Iran's internet has been shut down, exposing civilian risks. The humanitarian situation in Iran is deteriorating rapidly.

event_signals.headline: EU leaders split over US-Israeli war against Iran
event_signals.summary: European leaders are divided over their response to the US-Israeli war against Iran. Some EU members support the operation while others, led by France, push for restraint and humanitarian protection. The split complicates EU foreign policy coordination.

event_signals.headline: US allies and rivals in Asia gauge fallout from war in the Middle East
event_signals.summary: US allies and rivals across Asia are assessing the cascading economic and security fallout from the Middle East war. Japan and South Korea face acute energy vulnerability given their dependence on Gulf oil transiting Hormuz. China sees strategic opportunity. India balances its Iranian energy imports against US alignment.

event_signals.headline: Canada: Ontario budget coming March 26 — tariff relief and worker support
event_signals.summary: Ontario budget to be tabled March 26. Canada-Ontario funding is being geared to help workers laid off due to Trump tariffs. Doug Ford announced Billy Bishop airport expropriation. Trump made 'snarky dig' at Canada's PM, maintaining trade tensions.

event_signals.headline: UK PM Starmer leaked memo reveals power to override Scotland and Wales
event_signals.summary: A leaked memo revealed UK PM Keir Starmer's ministers can override devolved governments in Wales and Scotland on certain policy areas, generating political backlash in the devolved nations.

event_signals.headline: New Zealand Health Minister Shane Reti retires — Air NZ future questioned
event_signals.summary: New Zealand Health Minister Shane Reti announced his retirement from politics. Separately, ministers were grilled on the future of Air New Zealand amid bleak economic forecasts, reflecting broader economic challenges facing the Pacific nation.

event_signals.headline: Nepal undergoes generational shift in democracy
event_signals.summary: Analysis highlights a generational shift in Nepal's democratic politics, with younger leaders and parties challenging the established order. This transition could reshape Nepal's governance landscape and its foreign policy balance between India and China.

event_signals.headline: UK's HMS Dragon crew departs for Gulf deployment amid Iran conflict
event_signals.summary: Tearful relatives of HMS Dragon crew described 'scary and emotional' farewells as the Royal Navy destroyer departed for deployment to the Gulf region amid the Iran conflict. The deployment signals UK military involvement in the crisis.

event_signals.headline: Middle East conflict impact on India: banks, jobs, and remittances at risk
event_signals.summary: Analysis highlights the cascading impact of the Middle East war on India through three channels: banking sector exposure to Gulf clients, risk to millions of Indian workers in Gulf states, and potential disruption to $100B+ annual remittance flows from the GCC region.

event_signals.headline: Trucking executives warn fuel spike hitting fleets fast as gas prices skyrocket
event_signals.summary: US trucking industry executives warned that the fuel price spike from the Middle East conflict is hitting transportation fleets rapidly, with pass-through costs expected to reach consumers within weeks. Georgia Governor Kemp taking a 'wait and see' approach to suspending fuel taxes.

event_signals.headline: CBS restores financial ties with US Treasury for first time since 2011
event_signals.summary: CBS (Central Bank of Syria / possibly a different CBS) restored financial ties with the US Treasury for the first time since 2011. This may relate to Ahmed al-Sharaa's new Syrian government normalizing relations with the West.

event_signals.headline: Beyond 'Peak China' narrative — analysis argues economy reaching higher stage
event_signals.summary: Analysis challenges the 'Peak China' narrative, arguing China's economy is transitioning to a higher stage of development focused on technology, AI, and domestic consumption rather than declining.

event_signals.headline: IATA reports air cargo enabled $157B in frontloaded trade amid tariff uncertainty
event_signals.summary: IATA reported that air cargo supported $157 billion in frontloaded trade in 2025, driven by companies rushing to import goods before tariff increases. The data also showed air cargo supporting AI-related growth in technology supply chains.

event_signals.headline: GBP/EUR forecast highlights UK risks as pound buying levels remain elevated
event_signals.summary: Analysis highlights best GBP buying levels against the euro but warns of mounting UK economic risks including energy costs and fiscal pressures from the global crisis.

event_signals.headline: Demand for minerals to power technology could triple by 2030 — UN political chief
event_signals.summary: The UN's political chief stated that demand for critical minerals needed for technology and energy transition could triple by 2030, intensifying competition for resources among major powers.

event_signals.headline: Republican congressman urges Bessent to guard against Chinese investment in US
event_signals.summary: A Republican lawmaker urged Treasury Secretary Bessent to increase scrutiny of Chinese investment in the United States, reflecting ongoing bipartisan concern about Chinese economic influence and potential security risks.

event_signals.headline: India-EFTA trade pact marks two years — India deepening European trade ties
event_signals.summary: The India-EFTA (Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein) trade agreement marked its second anniversary. India is deepening European trade ties as part of its diversification strategy away from over-reliance on any single trade partner.

event_signals.headline: Zimbabwe RBZ tightens grip on mobile money amid currency pressures
event_signals.summary: Zimbabwe's Reserve Bank tightened regulations on mobile money platforms amid currency pressures, reflecting ongoing macroeconomic instability in the southern African nation.

event_signals.headline: Conrad Energy approves Mako Gas Project FID offshore Indonesia
event_signals.summary: Conrad Energy approved the final investment decision for the Mako Gas Project offshore Indonesia, adding new gas production capacity to Southeast Asia amid global energy supply concerns.

event_signals.headline: Trial opens for four men charged in Haiti President Moïse assassination
event_signals.summary: A trial opened in Miami for four men charged in the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse, bringing the long-running case to a US courtroom.

event_signals.headline: Anthropic vows to sue Pentagon over risk designation
event_signals.summary: AI company Anthropic threatened to sue the Pentagon over a risk designation, highlighting growing tensions between the US military and the technology sector over AI governance and defense applications.

event_signals.headline: Israel police officer files contempt motion against Ben-Gvir
event_signals.summary: An Israeli police officer filed a contempt motion against National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, reflecting ongoing internal Israeli political tensions even as the country is at war with Iran.

event_signals.headline: Lebanon parliamentary extension exceeds exceptional circumstances — constitutional challenge looming
event_signals.summary: Lebanese politician Bassil argued that parliament's term extension has exceeded the exceptional circumstances justifying it and is subject to constitutional appeal. Lebanon's governance crisis deepens amid the Hezbollah-Israel conflict.

event_signals.headline: Nigeria: Matawalle welcomes Gov Lawal to APC — party realignment continues
event_signals.summary: Nigerian Defence Minister Matawalle welcomed Zamfara State Governor Lawal to the ruling APC party, part of ongoing political realignment in Nigeria's northwest amid security challenges.

event_signals.headline: Former Secretary of State accuses Trump of folding under Israeli pressure on Iran
event_signals.summary: A former US Secretary of State accused Trump of 'folding under the same Israeli pressure that Obama reportedly resisted,' suggesting the Iran war was driven by Israeli influence rather than US strategic interests.

event_signals.headline: US proposes SAVE America Act amid contentious midterm politics
event_signals.summary: Trump pushed GOP lawmakers on the SAVE America Act voting bill, tying it to DHS funding restoration. The White House simultaneously instructed House GOP members to stop rhetoric on mass deportations. The administration is attempting a legislative course correction ahead of midterms.

event_signals.headline: Syrian leader Ahmed al-Sharaa emerges — CBS restores Treasury ties
event_signals.summary: Ahmed al-Sharaa (formerly Abu Mohammed al-Julani of HTS) is consolidating power as Syria's new leader. The Central Bank of Syria reportedly restored financial ties with the US Treasury for the first time since 2011, signaling Western recognition and normalization of the post-Assad government.

event_signals.headline: US faces financial crisis warning as Washington overlooks mounting debt
event_signals.summary: Analysis warns the US faces a financial crisis as Washington overlooks mounting national debt. War spending adds to already elevated deficits. The combination of tax cuts, defense spending, and economic disruption from the oil shock compounds fiscal pressures.

event_signals.headline: International Motors reports $130M tariff hit amid Class 8 rebound
event_signals.summary: International Motors (formerly Navistar) reported a $130 million tariff impact on Q4 results, quantifying the direct cost of Trump tariffs on a major US industrial company even as the Class 8 truck market rebounds.

event_signals.headline: Kraken crypto exchange gains access to Federal Reserve payment system
event_signals.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken gained access to the Federal Reserve payment system, a significant milestone for crypto-traditional finance integration. Ripple is also awaiting similar approval.

event_signals.headline: West African products markets surge amid Middle East conflict fallout
event_signals.summary: West African refined products markets surged as stakeholders assessed the fallout from the Middle East conflict on energy supply chains. The region faces particular vulnerability due to limited refining capacity and dependence on imported refined products.

event_signals.headline: New FATF report highlights illicit finance risks linked to offshore VASPs
event_signals.summary: A new FATF report highlighted illicit finance risks linked to offshore Virtual Asset Service Providers (VASPs), potentially signaling tighter regulation of cryptocurrency platforms operating from low-regulation jurisdictions.

event_signals.headline: Iran War Internet Shutdown exposes civilian risks of digital warfare
event_signals.summary: Iran's internet has been shut down during the war, cutting off civilian communications and exposing the risks of internet shutdowns during military conflicts. The blackout prevents independent reporting on civilian casualties and humanitarian conditions.

event_signals.headline: Alberta First Nations leaders pressure UCP to denounce separatism
event_signals.summary: First Nations leaders gathered at the Alberta legislature to pressure the ruling UCP party to denounce separatist movements, while polls showed majority of Albertans support restricting temporary immigrant access to healthcare.

event_signals.headline: Protests across Iran amid US strikes — 'hearing the street'
event_signals.summary: Reports from Iranian streets suggest mixed public sentiment — some protests against the regime amid the war, though the internet shutdown makes verification difficult. The war's impact on Iranian society is generating pressure both for and against the government.

event_signals.headline: Australian fuel price pain from Middle East conflict — when relief expected
event_signals.summary: Australian consumers face sustained fuel price increases from the Middle East conflict. Analysis suggests relief will depend on Hormuz reopening timeline, with potential for months of elevated prices.

event_signals.headline: Smartmatic says Trump 'campaign of retribution' driving prosecution
event_signals.summary: Election technology company Smartmatic alleged that Trump's 'campaign of retribution' is driving its prosecution, adding to concerns about politicization of the US justice system.

event_signals.headline: Irish deputy premier criticises fuel price 'anomalies' amid Middle East crisis
event_signals.summary: Ireland's deputy premier criticised fuel price 'anomalies,' suggesting price gouging by fuel companies amid the Middle East crisis. Multiple EU countries considering fuel tax suspensions or price controls.

event_signals.headline: IRS and Treasury propose digital asset broker regulations
event_signals.summary: The IRS and Treasury Department proposed new broker regulations for digital asset transactions, part of the ongoing effort to bring cryptocurrency under the traditional financial regulatory framework.

event_signals.headline: Tibet 1959 uprising anniversary — why it still echoes
event_signals.summary: Commentary on the 67th anniversary of the 1959 Tibetan uprising highlighted continued relevance of Tibetan self-determination issues amid China's tightening control over the region.

event_signals.headline: Indian Army pays tribute to soldier killed during training exercise
event_signals.summary: The Indian Army paid tribute to Akshit Sharma, who lost his life during a training exercise, highlighting the ongoing operational tempo and risks of India's large military establishment.

event_signals.headline: Mamata Banerjee meets former West Bengal Governor after five-day dharna protest
event_signals.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee met with former Governor CV Ananda Bose after concluding a five-day dharna (protest sit-in) against the central government's SIR policy, reflecting ongoing federal-state tensions in Indian politics.

event_signals.headline: Ecuadorean troops find 35m narco-submarine hidden in nature reserve
event_signals.summary: Ecuadorean military forces discovered a 35-meter long 'narco-submarine' hidden in a nature reserve, highlighting the scale of narcotics trafficking infrastructure in the country's ongoing security crisis.

event_signals.headline: Prolonged Iran conflict increases tourism risk globally
event_signals.summary: Analysis warns that the prolonged Iran conflict is increasing risk to global tourism, particularly in the Eastern Mediterranean and Middle East regions. Turkey, Egypt, Greece, and Gulf states face potential tourism revenue declines.

event_signals.headline: Pakistan: MK Stalin endgame as Tamil Nadu CM — Indian state politics heating up
event_signals.summary: BJP leader Piyush Goyal claimed MK Stalin's endgame as Tamil Nadu chief minister has begun, with Udhayanidhi his biggest internal threat within the DMK party. Indian state-level political dynamics continue to shift.

event_signals.headline: Mississippi and Georgia special elections underway — key 2026 midterm tests
event_signals.summary: Special elections in Mississippi (Bennie Thompson's seat) and Georgia (Marjorie Taylor Greene's successor) serve as early tests of the 2026 midterm political environment. Both are watched as bellwethers for the November elections.

event_signals.headline: Immigrant truck drivers vital to US economy — ICE crackdown forcing them off roads
event_signals.summary: An ICE crackdown on immigrant truck drivers is disrupting the US trucking workforce at a time when the industry is already stressed by surging fuel costs from the Middle East crisis. Immigrant drivers represent a significant portion of the long-haul trucking workforce.

event_signals.headline: Wisconsin lawmakers propose ranked choice voting for all elections
event_signals.summary: Wisconsin lawmakers introduced a proposal for ranked choice voting in all elections, part of a broader movement across US states to reform electoral systems.

event_signals.headline: Maine Democrat-led crackdown on deepfake AI campaign ads
event_signals.summary: Maine lawmakers passed legislation cracking down on deepfake AI-generated campaign advertisements, part of growing state-level efforts to regulate AI in political campaigning ahead of the 2026 midterms.

event_signals.headline: [chokepoint_disruption] Strait of Hormuz in state of 'effective closure' since March 2, 2026. IRGC officially closed the strait and began laying
event_signals.summary: Strait of Hormuz in state of 'effective closure' since March 2, 2026. IRGC officially closed the strait and began laying naval mines. Daily transits collapsed from 138 ships to fewer than 15 (90% reduction), with only 2 Iranian-flagged vessels passing on March 8. Approximately 20,000 seafarers trapped inside Persian Gulf. Maersk, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM suspended all vessel crossings. Maritime traffic has been at virtual standstill since March 2. GPS spoofing and AIS jamming reported. Several commercial ships sustained missile and drone strikes with fatalities reported as recently as March 6. Cargo rerouted to Salalah (Oman), Yanbu (Saudi Arabia), or around Cape of Good Hope. Pre-conflict: ~13 million barrels/day crude transited (31% of all seaborne crude flows). Roughly 20% of world oil and natural gas normally passes through strait.

event_signals.headline: [chokepoint_disruption] US Navy informed commercial shipping that Hormuz escorts are 'not possible for now,' leaving tankers without military pr
event_signals.summary: US Navy informed commercial shipping that Hormuz escorts are 'not possible for now,' leaving tankers without military protection. Trump proposed having the US Navy escort tankers and told CBS he is 'thinking about taking it [Hormuz] over.' Insurance providers largely cancelled coverage for strait transits. War-risk surcharges implemented. VLCC daily charter rates jumped from ~$50,000 to over $400,000. France, Netherlands, and India are discussing defensive naval escort missions but no operations commenced as of March 10.

event_signals.headline: [chokepoint_disruption] Asian countries disproportionately affected by Hormuz closure — over 80% of oil and LNG transiting the strait is bound f
event_signals.summary: Asian countries disproportionately affected by Hormuz closure — over 80% of oil and LNG transiting the strait is bound for Asia. 55 Chinese-flagged ships trapped in Persian Gulf; only 2 Chinese transits since March 1 (vs. 49 in prior week). China receives 40% of its oil and 30% of its LNG through the strait. South Asia faces acute LNG disruption: Qatar and UAE account for 99% of Pakistan's LNG imports, 72% of Bangladesh's, and 53% of India's. Indian export cargo worth ~$40 million to Iran stranded at Kandla and Mundra ports. Iraq shutting down major oil field production due to storage capacity limits.

event_signals.headline: [chokepoint_disruption] Dual chokepoint crisis: Houthi-controlled Yemen announced it would resume attacks on Red Sea shipping targeting Israel a
event_signals.summary: Dual chokepoint crisis: Houthi-controlled Yemen announced it would resume attacks on Red Sea shipping targeting Israel and commercial vessels, following the US-Israeli strikes on Iran starting February 28. Shipping companies that had begun returning services to Red Sea/Suez route are now expected to reverse course. BIMCO stated war risk premiums likely to increase sharply. Plans for phased return of container shipping to Red Sea in 2026 shelved. Combined with Hormuz closure, both Middle East maritime chokepoints are effectively shut — unprecedented in modern shipping history. Jebel Ali (DP World's 15.5M TEU hub) cut off from global ocean trade.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] Trump announced March 10 that the US will lift some sanctions on oil-producing countries to keep energy prices down amid
event_signals.summary: Trump announced March 10 that the US will lift some sanctions on oil-producing countries to keep energy prices down amid the Iran war. Exact quote: 'So, we have sanctions on some countries. We're going to take those sanctions off until this straightens out. Then, who knows, maybe we won't have to put them on — there'll be so much peace.' Countries with oil sector sanctions include Russia, Iran, and Venezuela. No specific countries or executive orders named yet. Represents potential major reversal of sanctions policy driven by oil price crisis (Brent surged to $116.50/bbl).

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC issued General License 133 on March 5, 2026, authorizing delivery and sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum produ
event_signals.summary: OFAC issued General License 133 on March 5, 2026, authorizing delivery and sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products to India. The 30-day waiver (March 5 - April 4, 2026) applies to cargo loaded before March 5. Treasury Secretary Bessent cited concerns about global energy supply pressures. This effectively permits Russian oil shipments to India that would otherwise be sanctioned, a significant concession in the context of the energy crisis.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] EU designated the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation on February 19, 2026, under Commo
event_signals.summary: EU designated the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation on February 19, 2026, under Common Position 2001/931/CFSP. All IRGC funds and financial assets in EU member states frozen. Decision followed political agreement on January 29, 2026, with support from Italy, Spain, and France. EU also imposed new sanctions on 6 entities and 15 individuals in Iran, including Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni, Prosecutor General Mohammad Movahedi-Azad, and presiding judge Iman Afshari for violent repression of protests. Marks the end of the EU's long strategy of engagement with Iran.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC sanctioned architects of Iran's brutal crackdown on peaceful protests under direction of President Trump. Treasury
event_signals.summary: OFAC sanctioned architects of Iran's brutal crackdown on peaceful protests under direction of President Trump. Treasury targeted Iranian weapons procurement networks supporting ballistic missile and military aircraft programs. Additional sanctions on shadow banking networks allowing Iran's elite to launder revenue from natural resources. These represent intensification of the 'maximum pressure' campaign concurrent with military operations.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] EU sanctioned additional 41 vessels of Russia's shadow fleet (announced December 18, 2025, now in effect). Vessels subje
event_signals.summary: EU sanctioned additional 41 vessels of Russia's shadow fleet (announced December 18, 2025, now in effect). Vessels subject to port access ban and ban on providing maritime transport services. Total designated vessels now near 600. EU also renewed Ukraine-related sanctions targeting individuals until March 7, 2027. Economic sanctions against Russia extended until July 31, 2026. EU Commission announced plans for 20th sanctions package covering energy, financial services, and trade. EU listed additional 8 individuals for human rights violations in Russia (February 23, 2026).

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC amended Russia-related General License 129A on March 5, 2026, regarding Rosneft Deutschland GmbH and RN Refining &
event_signals.summary: OFAC amended Russia-related General License 129A on March 5, 2026, regarding Rosneft Deutschland GmbH and RN Refining & Marketing GmbH. Removed the April 29, 2026 expiration date, making the authorization indefinite. This effectively allows continued German operations of Rosneft subsidiaries without time limit.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] Russian shadow fleet tanker M/V TRUST (already blacklisted by US, EU, UK) conducted covert 'semi-dark' ship-to-ship oil
event_signals.summary: Russian shadow fleet tanker M/V TRUST (already blacklisted by US, EU, UK) conducted covert 'semi-dark' ship-to-ship oil transfer of ~325,000 barrels of crude oil (~$29.3M) in Gulf of Oman on March 3, 2026. The vessel loaded at Ust-Luga (EU-sanctioned Russian export terminal) 38 days prior. Transfer occurred in Omani territorial waters; receiving vessel had AIS disabled. Operation exploited regional instability from Iran war for reduced scrutiny. Demonstrates continued Russian sanctions evasion using advanced GPS spoofing and AIS jamming techniques.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC issued General License 51 on March 6, 2026, authorizing Venezuelan-origin gold transactions including importation,
event_signals.summary: OFAC issued General License 51 on March 6, 2026, authorizing Venezuelan-origin gold transactions including importation, refining, and resale by established US entities. Six new FAQs clarifying Venezuela-related GLs 48, 49, 50A, and 47 also published. Represents easing of Venezuela gold sector restrictions.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC sanctioned Rwanda Defence Force (RDF) and four senior officials on March 2, 2026, under EO 13413 for supporting M23
event_signals.summary: OFAC sanctioned Rwanda Defence Force (RDF) and four senior officials on March 2, 2026, under EO 13413 for supporting M23 armed group in DRC and violating the Washington Accords for Peace (signed December 4, 2025). Officials designated: Vincent Nyakarundi (Army Chief of Staff), Ruki Karusisi (5th Infantry Division Commander), Mubarakh Muganga (Chief of Defence Staff), Stanislas Gashugi (Special Operations Force Commander). Asset freeze and prohibition on US transactions.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC lifted sanctions on three Malian officials on February 27, 2026: Defense Minister Sadio Camara, Air Force Chief of
event_signals.summary: OFAC lifted sanctions on three Malian officials on February 27, 2026: Defense Minister Sadio Camara, Air Force Chief of Staff Alou Boi Diarra, and Air Force Deputy Chief of Staff Adama Bagayoko. Originally sanctioned July 24, 2023 for facilitating Wagner Group activities. Removal reflects improved US-Mali relations following Wagner's withdrawal from Mali in June 2025.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] Globe Trekkers LLC, a UAE-based freight and logistics company, removed from SDN List on March 6, 2026. Originally design
event_signals.summary: Globe Trekkers LLC, a UAE-based freight and logistics company, removed from SDN List on March 6, 2026. Originally designated November 2, 2023, for shipping goods to Russia. No rationale provided for removal.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] OFAC announced Counter Terrorism Designations and Sudan-related Designation Update on March 9, 2026. EU also added four
event_signals.summary: OFAC announced Counter Terrorism Designations and Sudan-related Designation Update on March 9, 2026. EU also added four RSF (Rapid Support Forces) commanders/leaders to sanctions list in February 2026, reflecting UN Security Council designations.

event_signals.headline: [tariff_change] SCOTUS ruled 6-3 on February 20, 2026 (Learning Resources Inc. v. Trump) that IEEPA does not authorize presidential tari
event_signals.summary: SCOTUS ruled 6-3 on February 20, 2026 (Learning Resources Inc. v. Trump) that IEEPA does not authorize presidential tariffs. All IEEPA tariffs invalidated. Trump responded with Section 122 proclamation imposing 10% global tariff effective February 24, 2026, for 150 days (expires July 24, 2026). Trump announced intent to raise to 15% (maximum allowed under Section 122) but formal order not yet published. Trade-weighted average US tariff rate at 13.0% under Section 122 at 15%.

event_signals.headline: [tariff_change] US Court of International Trade Judge Eaton issued nationwide order on March 4, 2026, requiring CBP to begin refund proc
event_signals.summary: US Court of International Trade Judge Eaton issued nationwide order on March 4, 2026, requiring CBP to begin refund process for IEEPA tariffs. Estimated $166-175 billion in refunds owed to over 330,000 companies. Major companies filing suits include Apple ($3-3.3B potential refund), Bausch & Lomb, Dyson, FedEx, L'Oreal, Hasbro, Mattel. Judge suspended immediate compliance order and directed government status update by March 12. Monthly interest accruing at ~$700M.

event_signals.headline: [tariff_change] Section 122 tariffs on Canada received 30-day suspension, took effect March 4. On March 5, Trump exempted auto imports f
event_signals.summary: Section 122 tariffs on Canada received 30-day suspension, took effect March 4. On March 5, Trump exempted auto imports from tariffs until April 2. On March 6, imports covered by USMCA trade deal exempted. USMCA-compliant goods of Canada and Mexico not subject to the Section 122 tariff. Net effect: Canada and Mexico largely exempt from new tariff regime for qualifying goods.

event_signals.headline: [chokepoint_disruption] Maersk issued emergency freight increase for world to/from UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia (Dammam and Jubail), Bahrain, Kuwait
event_signals.summary: Maersk issued emergency freight increase for world to/from UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia (Dammam and Jubail), Bahrain, Kuwait, Iraq and Oman (Sohar) effective March 2. European natural gas prices surged from €30/MWh to over €60/MWh (doubled in one week) by March 3. Oil prices exceeded $100/bbl on March 8, peaking at $126. G7 finance ministers signaled coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve release to stabilize markets. Iraq shutting down major oil field production due to storage capacity limits with export routes blocked.

event_signals.headline: [wto_dispute] WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) scheduled for March 26-29, 2026, in Yaounde, Cameroon. Key agenda items include
event_signals.summary: WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) scheduled for March 26-29, 2026, in Yaounde, Cameroon. Key agenda items include dispute settlement reform, food security, fisheries subsidies, and e-commerce duties. WTO dispute settlement system remains bifurcated: 58 members use Multi-Party Interim Appeal Arbitration Arrangement (binding) while US continues to block Appellate Body appointments. Reform consultations to continue after MC14. The conference has not yet taken place.

event_signals.headline: [sanction_change] UK planning secondary legislation establishing end-use controls requiring export licenses for goods posing diversion ris
event_signals.summary: UK planning secondary legislation establishing end-use controls requiring export licenses for goods posing diversion risks to Russia. UK's Office of Financial Sanctions Implementation (OFSI) investigating first five potential cyber sanctions breaches, all involving financial services firms. Maximum penalties: £1M civil fines or 50% breach value; criminal liability up to 7 years. New Belarus guidance on trade license considerations also issued.

event_signals.headline: [trade_agreement] India approved limited easing of restrictions on Chinese foreign direct investment, partially reversing curbs imposed af
event_signals.summary: India approved limited easing of restrictions on Chinese foreign direct investment, partially reversing curbs imposed after the 2020 Galwan border clash. Signals cautious diplomatic thaw driven by India's need for Chinese manufacturing investment and technology. Chinese tech companies may resume India operations.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] 2026 US-Israel-Iran War (Operation Epic Fury)
event_signals.summary: US-Israeli joint strikes on Iran began Feb 28 under Operation Epic Fury (US) and Operation Roaring Lion (Israel). ~900 strikes in first 12 hours. As of Day 11 (March 10): over 3,000 targets struck, 43 Iranian warships destroyed. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated Feb 28. Mojtaba Khamenei elected replacement March 8. US death toll: 7 service members (6 killed in Iranian strike on makeshift ops center in Kuwait on March 2, 1 died from injuries sustained in Saudi Arabia). Iranian death toll: 1,332+. Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones. US lost 11 MQ-9 Reaper drones. $5.6B in US munitions used in first 2 days. Pentagon shifting to cheaper laser-guided bombs. Trump vowed March 10 would be 'most intense day of strikes'. War powers vote failed in Senate March 4, allowing strikes to continue. Military draft acknowledged as 'on the table'.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] 2026 US-Israel-Iran War - Nuclear Facilities Struck
event_signals.summary: US-Israeli strikes targeted Iranian nuclear facilities: Natanz enrichment facility (entrance buildings damaged, IAEA confirmed no radiological release), Isfahan nuclear complex, secret underground site 'Minzadehei' near Tehran (where Iran relocated nuclear weapons program after June 2025 Israeli campaign, destroyed by IAF), and Lavisan 2/Mojdeh complex laboratory. Four separate nuclear facility strikes confirmed during the campaign. IAEA urged restraint. Arms Control Association assessed Iran's nuclear program did NOT pose an imminent threat prior to strikes.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] 2026 Strait of Hormuz Crisis
event_signals.summary: Iran began laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz (confirmed March 10 by CNN). IRGC officially confirmed strait closure March 2. US destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying naval vessels. Iran retains 80-90% of its small naval vessels and mine-laying craft, enabling deployment of hundreds more mines. Major shipping companies (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd) suspended transits. Insurance premiums spiking. Tanker traffic plummeted. ~20% of global crude oil normally transits Hormuz. This is the most severe disruption to global energy supply chains in decades.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] 2026 Kurdish Rebellion in Iran
event_signals.summary: Kurdish fighters (PJAK - Kurdistan Free Life Party) launched ground offensive into western Iran from Iraq starting March 2. CIA working to arm Kurdish forces to spark uprising (reported March 3). Mossad-CIA joint effort to support Kurdish factions. PJAK fighters positioned around Mariwan mountains in western Iran. IRGC struck Kurdish positions with dozens of drones. Iran threatened to target 'all facilities' in Kurdistan Region of Iraq (March 6). Trump reversed course March 7-8, saying he does not want Kurds entering the war. Uncertain whether Kurdish operations continue.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] Russia-Ukraine War
event_signals.summary: Day ~1110 of war. Russia captured 2,038 sq mi in the past year (0.9% of Ukraine territory) but momentum shifting. Ukraine recovered 244 sq km (94 sq mi) in southern Ukraine since January. Russian territorial gains in February hit 20-month low. Russian casualties: ~1,274,990 total since Feb 2022 (~950/day recent pace). Russian missile strike on Kharkiv apartment killed 10 including 2 children (March 7) using new Izdeliye-30 cruise missile. Ukrainian forces damaged Russian frigate and warships at Novorossiysk naval base (March 1-2). POW swap: 200 each side (March 5). Peace negotiations stalled over territorial demands. Russia insists on remaining 20% of Donetsk.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] Syria-Lebanon-Hezbollah Tensions
event_signals.summary: Syria deployed hundreds of soldiers, tanks, troop carriers, and field artillery to Lebanese border (Qusair region near Hermel) where Hezbollah weapon stores believed located. Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa declared support for Lebanese President Joseph Aoun in disarming Hezbollah. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam announced ban on Hezbollah military activities. Artillery shells from Lebanon hit town of Serghaya (20km from Damascus) — Syrian army accused Hezbollah. IDF expanding southern Lebanon positions but no major invasion planned until Iran situation calms. Syria brought in elite troops including Uzbek and Uighur fighters.

event_signals.headline: [nuclear_event] US Minuteman III ICBM Test (GT-255)
event_signals.summary: US Air Force test-fired unarmed Minuteman III ICBM (GT-255) from Vandenberg Space Force Base on March 3 at 11:01 PM PT. Equipped with two test re-entry vehicles. Flew 4,200 miles to Kwajalein Atoll, Marshall Islands. Launch triggered by E-6B Mercury 'Doomsday Plane'. Air Force Global Strike Command supervised. Officials stressed test was pre-scheduled (not Iran-related), but timing amid Operation Epic Fury was noted as deliberate signaling by analysts.

event_signals.headline: [nuclear_event] US-China Nuclear Test Accusations
event_signals.summary: US accused China of conducting secret nuclear explosive test on June 22, 2020, at Lop Nur test site (2.75 magnitude seismic event detected in Kazakhstan). Allegation made Feb 6 by Undersecretary Thomas DiNanno at Conference on Disarmament in Geneva. First direct US accusation of CTBT violation by China. US also alleges China preparing for future tests with yields in hundreds of tons range. Satellite imagery shows expanded facilities and new tunnels at Lop Nur. China denied: called accusation 'completely groundless' and 'fabrication of pretexts for US resumption of nuclear tests'. CTBTO says evidence inconclusive. US said it would 'match' alleged Chinese low-yield tests.

event_signals.headline: [nuclear_event] North Korea Cruise Missile Tests and Ballistic Launch
event_signals.summary: Kim Jong Un supervised test launch of strategic cruise missiles from new destroyer Choe Hyon at Nampho Shipyard (March 4-5). Kim stated navy nuclear arming progressing. Directed construction of 2 surface warships of Choe Hyon class or higher per year under new 5-year military plan beginning 2026. Separately, North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles toward Yellow Sea on March 10 in response to start of US-ROK Freedom Shield exercise.

event_signals.headline: [arms_deal] UAE Patriot System Contract
event_signals.summary: Raytheon Co. awarded $183.7M firm-fixed-price contract for Patriot missile system hardware and services for United Arab Emirates under Foreign Military Sales program.

event_signals.headline: [arms_deal] US-Saudi $142B Arms Deal (ongoing implementation)
event_signals.summary: The $142B US-Saudi defense cooperation agreement signed May 2025 (called 'largest defense cooperation agreement in US history') continues implementation. Covers 5 categories: air force/space, air/missile defense, maritime/coastal security, border security, communications upgrades. Actual notifications to date: $34.6B (2017-2025). Deal originally framed as part of $600B Saudi investment package. Experts note actual deliveries likely to fall well short of headline figure, as occurred with Trump's first Saudi deal.

event_signals.headline: [military_exercise] Freedom Shield 2026
event_signals.summary: US-South Korea annual defensive military exercise commenced March 9, runs through March 19 (10 days). ~18,000 Korean troops participating. Exercise proceeding despite US forces simultaneously engaged in Iran war. North Korea responded with ballistic missile launches on March 10.

event_signals.headline: [military_exercise] NATO Steadfast Dart 2026
event_signals.summary: NATO's largest military exercise of 2026. Ran from Jan 2 to March 18 across Central Europe (Germany/Baltic Sea). Second deployment of the Allied Reaction Force (ARF). Tests Alliance readiness and cohesion in simulated near-peer conflict scenario.

event_signals.headline: [military_exercise] Cobra Gold 2026 (45th iteration)
event_signals.summary: Indo-Pacific multinational exercise, 45th iteration. Late February to early March 2026. Over 8,000 military personnel from 30 nations. Provides tactical, scenario-based instruction for multiple contingencies.

event_signals.headline: [military_exercise] Justified Accord 2026 (East Africa)
event_signals.summary: Joint all-domain exercise across Djibouti, Kenya, and Tanzania. Feb 23 - March 13, 2026. Led by SETAF-AF. ~1,500 personnel. Building readiness against shared security threats in East Africa.

event_signals.headline: [military_exercise] USS George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group Certification
event_signals.summary: USS George H.W. Bush carrier strike group completed COMPTUEX (Composite Training Unit Exercise) on March 5, certifying and readying the CSG for deployment. Deployment timing notable given Middle East operations.

event_signals.headline: [cyber_event] Iranian Cyber Retaliation Campaign
event_signals.summary: Following US-Israeli strikes (Operation Epic Fury), Iran launched multi-vector retaliatory cyber campaign. Attacks on AWS data centers in UAE and Bahrain observed. IRGC-linked 'Muddy Water' APT conducting 'Operation Olalampo' targeting Middle East, Turkey, Africa. Techniques overlap with RedKitten campaign indicating coordinated infrastructure across Iranian-aligned actors. Surge of ~60 hacktivist groups active as of March 2. 149 hacktivist DDoS attacks hit 110 organizations in 16 countries. Keymous+, DieNet, and NoName057(16) account for 74.6% of all activity.

event_signals.headline: [cyber_event] Russian APT28 Surveillance of Ukrainian Military
event_signals.summary: Russian state-sponsored hacking group APT28 (Fancy Bear) observed using implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long-term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel. Part of ongoing Russian cyber operations accompanying kinetic war.

event_signals.headline: [cyber_event] Chinese State-Sponsored Cyber Campaigns (Volt/Salt Typhoon)
event_signals.summary: Chinese state-sponsored groups continue major campaigns in 2026. Volt Typhoon infiltrating US critical infrastructure networks. Salt Typhoon targeting US telecom providers (AT&T, Verizon), stealing metadata and compromising communications of political figures. Both campaigns represent persistent, long-term intelligence collection operations.

event_signals.headline: [conflict_escalation] Houthi Red Sea Attack Resumption
event_signals.summary: Houthis announced Feb 28 they would resume attacks on Israel and commercial ships in Red Sea following US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Internal debate within Houthi leadership over response delayed action. By early March, Houthis fired ballistic missile at Israeli vessel in Red Sea. Oil tanker hit by drone boat in Strait of Hormuz area (March 2). Further escalation expected as Houthi attacks complement Iran's broader maritime disruption strategy.
